title: Study argues bigger launch vehicles may not always be better
summary: A new study suggests that extremely large launch vehicles may not always be the optimal choice for space missions. SpaceX and other companies are currently developing such massive rockets.

title: ESA to seek lunar mapping capability for Argonaut lander
summary: ESA will seek external lunar topographic data for the design and possibly the first mission of its Argonaut lunar lander. The agency aims to leverage existing lunar mapping capabilities to support this initiative.

title: Beck: Iridium acquisition the “logical next step” for Rocket Lab
summary: Rocket Lab's CEO describes the acquisition of Iridium as a strategic move to expand into the space services market. The deal is seen as a logical progression for the company's growth ambitions.

title: Discovering the Universe – Astrophysics Flagship Space Observatories Offer Insights to Key Questions
summary: BAE Systems is enabling cutting-edge technology for NASA's astrophysics flagship space observatories, aiding in the study of distant galaxies, exoplanets, and their atmospheres. The collaboration aims to expand humanity's knowledge of the universe.

title: Quantum computing is about to become a national security problem in orbit
summary: Quantum computing's rapid advancement poses a national security threat by potentially breaking modern encryption, directly impacting the commercial space industry.

title: Rocket Lab to acquire Iridium
summary: Rocket Lab is acquiring satellite telecommunications company Iridium for $8 billion to expand into end-to-end space services. This acquisition marks a significant step in Rocket Lab's growth strategy.

title: SatVu restarts commercial operations with HotSat-2
summary: SatVu has resumed commercial operations with its HotSat-2 satellite after its first satellite failed in 2023. This marks a return to revenue-generating activities for the British thermal imaging startup.

title: Craft Prospect selected for The Sunday Times Scotland Fast 50
summary: Glasgow-based space engineering company Craft Prospect has been included in The Sunday Times Scotland Fast 50, a new annual list recognizing fast-growing companies. This marks a significant achievement for the company in the Scottish space sector.

title: China establishes VLEO industry alliance as satellites demonstrate sustained low-orbit operations
summary: China has formed a national industry alliance focused on very low Earth orbit (VLEO) operations, as satellites demonstrate sustained activities below 300 kilometers and propulsion startups gain investment.

title: Rocket Lab launches 10th Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its 10th radar-imaging satellite for Synspective, a Japanese company, on June 26. The mission was delayed due to a previous responsive space flight.

title: 'The start of a new era': Rocket Lab buying satellite-communications company Iridium for $8 billion
summary: Rocket Lab is acquiring satellite-communications company Iridium for $8 billion, marking a significant expansion into the satellite industry. The deal highlights Rocket Lab's strategic move to bolster its presence in the space sector.

title: It's been 25 years since'A.I. Artificial Intelligence', and we think this was Spielberg at the top of his sci-fi game
summary: It marks the 25th anniversary of the film 'A.I. Artificial Intelligence', directed by Steven Spielberg. The movie is celebrated as a collaboration between two cinematic geniuses in sci-fi.

title: Watch 2 NASA astronauts fix ISS' huge robotic arm during June 30 spacewalk
summary: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir will perform a spacewalk on June 30 to repair the International Space Station's Canadarm2. The event will be broadcast live.

title: 'Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions' takes space exploration in a different (and friendlier) direction, and we chatted with System Era's creative director about it (interview)
summary: The follow-up to Astroneer, 'Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions,' offers a fresh and more accessible approach to space exploration. System Era's creative director discussed the game's unique direction in an interview.

title: NASA to launch rescue mission June 30 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere
summary: NASA plans to launch a rescue mission on June 30 to prevent the Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere. Northrop Grumman will deploy a rocket from an airplane to extend the telescope's orbit.

title: This star's light has been crossing space since the American Revolution. Here's how to find it
summary: A star's light that left its surface around the time of the American Revolution has been traveling through space and can now be observed. This provides a unique opportunity to study light from the late 18th century.

title: Light pollution may be erasing millions of dollars in value at US dark-sky parks
summary: Researchers found that light pollution is significantly reducing the economic value of US dark-sky parks by billions of dollars. They used satellite data and visitor surveys to quantify the impact on these protected areas.

title: Mars orbiter watches Perseverance rover cross the marathon finish line | Space photo of the day for June 29, 2026
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has completed a marathon distance on Mars, as observed by an orbiter. The rover continues to explore the Red Planet, raising hopes for even longer missions.

title: How NASA's 'America 250' celebrations are reaching from the sky to the moon
summary: NASA is celebrating America's 250th anniversary with events involving aircraft and spacecraft. The celebrations will take place on July 4, marking the ratification of the Declaration of Independence.

title: The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered a massive and densely packed galaxy cluster from the universe's early history, defying existing scientific expectations about their formation.

title: Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways Project Celebrates Student Innovation Through ROADS from Earth to Venus National Challenge
summary: The Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways (NESSP) project concluded its 2025–2026 ROADS from Earth to Venus National Challenge, engaging over 500 students in Venus exploration-inspired science and engineering activities.

title: NASA, SBA Announce New Initiative to Scale American Space Economy
summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) launched a new initiative to boost investment in American space economy manufacturers. The SBIC-NASA Initiative aims to support lunar and Martian exploration by funding critical technologies.

title: NASA’s Newest Wind Tunnel Builds on Legacy of Innovation
summary: NASA's new Flight Dynamics Research Facility (FDRF) at Langley Research Center replaces two aging wind tunnels, marking the agency's first major wind tunnel construction in over 40 years. This facility continues NASA's century-long legacy of advancing aeronautics research.

title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Preps for Spacewalk
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams prepares for a spacewalk by testing his spacesuit inside the ISS's Quest airlock with assistance from ESA's Sophie Adenot. The spacewalk is scheduled for June 2026.

title: Mapping Earth’s Observations, featuring Betsy Ford
summary: NASA's Earth-observing satellites track critical environmental phenomena like aerosols, soil moisture, and land-cover changes, providing essential data for science, policy, agriculture, and climate research. The Earth Science Division (ESD) manages this extensive portfolio.

title: NextSTEP-3 A: Lunar Enabling Technology
summary: NASA issued a draft Broad Agency Announcement under NextSTEP-3, Appendix A, to advance lunar surface and cislunar architecture technologies. The solicitation aims to close key technology gaps for lunar missions.

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input to Accelerate Lunar Surface Technologies
summary: NASA is collaborating with industry to accelerate the development of lunar surface technologies by seeking feedback on a draft solicitation. The goal is to enhance long-term lunar exploration through cohesive technology, infrastructure, and operations.

title: NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander Challenge
summary: NASA revealed the winners of the 2026 Human Lander Challenge, highlighting student-developed solutions for life support systems in lunar landers. The initiative supports the Artemis program's goal of returning astronauts to the Moon.

title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
summary: NASA is testing a new refueling device for future in-space missions, enabling spacecraft to refuel in Earth orbit before deep space exploration. The device functions like a gas pump nozzle for spacecraft fuel tanks.

title: Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission
summary: NASA and its partners are preparing for the June 30 launch of the Swift Boost Mission to raise the orbit of NASA's Swift observatory. This mission aims to extend the operational lifespan of the observatory.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is scheduled to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to extend the lifespan of a NASA space telescope. This marks the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to support future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights the telescope's capability to study dense star fields and its potential for future scientific missions.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work as part of the testing process.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate visitors about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's spaceflight schedule features six orbital launches, including the debut of the Pegasus XL rocket and concurrent missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese launchers. The Pegasus XL launch marks a significant milestone as it joins other high-profile missions in a busy launch period.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test flight took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 robotic arm as the CRS-34 cargo mission departs. The repairs are part of ongoing maintenance to ensure the arm's functionality for future operations.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket program, with its second stage now transported to Stennis Space Center. The company aims for a debut launch in 2026.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for space industry activities.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace aborted the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, though the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing operational risks as NASA approaches its 2030 extension deadline, with the risk margin for regular operations described as 'alarming' by a NASA advisory committee chair.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX alumni have collectively raised $9.2 billion in funding for their new ventures, highlighting the company's role as a talent incubator. The data underscores SpaceX's impact on fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among its former employees.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: A New Crop of Early Stage Space Startups Emerges
summary: A new group of early-stage space startups has been introduced. The article highlights the emergence of these innovative companies in the space industry.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to boost its space industry. This expansion aims to meet growing demand for launch services.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission focused on addressing hacking threats and shared-compute modules in rideshare satellites. Their collaboration aims to test Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution for on-orbit security challenges.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal this month for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO. This move aims to enhance Japan's sovereign satellite communication capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to advocate for the interests of NGSO operators in the growing satellite industry.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the emerging reentry sector. The event highlights growing competition among startups in space reentry technology.

title: New Megaconstellation Startup Eclipse Space Targets Sovereign Market
summary: Eclipse Space, a new megaconstellation startup founded by SpaceX alumni, has emerged from stealth mode to target sovereign governments and enterprises for constellation ownership. The company aims to make satellite constellation ownership accessible to sovereign markets.

title: Saudia and Neo Space Group Linefit Inaugural Airbus A321XLR Aircraft With IFC
summary: Saudia and Neo Space Group have activated the first line-fit In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) system aboard Saudia's new Airbus A321XLR aircraft. The system is powered by NSG Skywaves and leverages SES's satellite network.

title: SiriusXM Continues Refresh With SXM-11 Launch
summary: SpaceX launched the SXM-11 satellite for SiriusXM, which will be the highest power satellite in SiriusXM’s fleet. This mission is part of SiriusXM's ongoing satellite refresh efforts.

title: Rocket Lab’s End-to-End Vision Comes Into Focus With ‘Transformative’ Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab announced a transformative deal to acquire Iridium Communications, advancing its goal of becoming an end-to-end space company. The acquisition integrates launch, manufacturing, and satellite operations under one organization.

title: Omar Qaise Looks to Build OQ Technology Into a Sovereign European Pioneer of D2D
summary: OQ Technology, led by CEO and founder Omar Qaise, aims to establish itself as a sovereign European leader in direct-to-device (D2D) technology amid growing market demand. The company seeks to become a trusted partner for European governments in this emerging sector.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted that commercial markets are prepared for Earth Observation (EO) solutions, but there is a persistent gap in understanding how to effectively communicate these solutions to customers. Industry experts emphasized the need to bridge this gap to fully leverage EO's potential.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Canadian satellite communications provider Galaxy Broadband for up to $52 million CAD ($36.6 million USD). The deal aims to expand Calian's satellite connectivity and remote solutions portfolio.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. FlexMaritime combines SES’s satellite network with integrated solutions.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured 4.6 million euro ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding for its 'OrbitCloud' R&D program aimed at developing a space-based edge computing network. The initiative is supported by Business Finland.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus received a $5 million award from NASA to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The technology will support NASA's future lunar and deep-space missions.
